mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of rate increase episodes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market cooling that reduces recession probability, prompting institutional investors to rotate into equities and drive positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in over 60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in housing starts indicates consumer spending resilience shifting toward services, which supports corporate earnings growth and lifts equity prices over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 50% of historical occurrences, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.4 percent month-over-month during periods of elevated inflation confirms supply-side capacity expansion, alleviating cost-push pressures and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300, with forward returns indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth directly expands aggregate household income and consumer demand, creating a persistent positive correlation with corporate revenue growth and forward equity performance.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 24-month windows, or positive payroll surprises coincide with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0 percent, real borrowing costs become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and consumer credit, triggering a risk-off equity selloff.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of periods following the 5.0 percent threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid GDP expansion combined with accelerating consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary risk premium, forcing portfolio de-risking and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a positive coefficient in out-of-sample testing, or 60-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of high-growth high-inflation quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in industrial production above the 100 index baseline signals manufacturing sector recovery, improving corporate profit margins and driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when industrial production crosses the 100 threshold.
